channel.title: Tech News Briefing
channel.description: Weekly tech news briefing covering AI, enterprise architecture, platform engineering, identity, payments, and cybersecurity.
channel.itunes:author: Matt O'Dell

item.title: Tech News Briefing — June 2, 2026
item.description: This week's dominant narrative is the cost and control crisis in enterprise AI tooling — Microsoft unveiled Project Polaris at Build 2026, canceled internal Claude Code licenses by June 30, while Uber revealed it burned through its entire 2026 AI tools budget in four months. Anthropic filed for IPO at a $965B post-money valuation the same week it launched Opus 4.8. Databricks entered cybersecurity with Lakewatch SIEM and Snowflake plans a $1B acquisition of Observe. Auth0 shipped its full agentic identity stack. Security had a brutal week: Cisco's sixth SD-WAN zero-day, a critical cPanel authentication bypass on CISA's exploited list, and the Verizon DBIR confirmed vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential theft as the number one breach vector for the first time.
item.itunes:summary: This week's dominant narrative is the cost and control crisis in enterprise AI tooling — Microsoft unveiled Project Polaris at Build 2026, canceled internal Claude Code licenses by June 30, while Uber revealed it burned through its entire 2026 AI tools budget in four months. Anthropic filed for IPO at a $965B post-money valuation the same week it launched Opus 4.8. Databricks entered cybersecurity with Lakewatch SIEM and Snowflake plans a $1B acquisition of Observe. Auth0 shipped its full agentic identity stack. Security had a brutal week: Cisco's sixth SD-WAN zero-day, a critical cPanel authentication bypass on CISA's exploited list, and the Verizon DBIR confirmed vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential theft as the number one breach vector for the first time.

item.title: Tech News Briefing — June 3, 2026
item.description: This week's tech landscape is shaped by three converging forces: massive AI capital consolidation, a landmark shift in enterprise AI leadership, and an accelerating identity-centric threat environment. Anthropic's confidential IPO filing arrives alongside its historic milestone of overtaking OpenAI in business adoption (34.4% vs. 32.3%), even as 79% of organizations report AI adoption challenges and GitHub's new usage-based Copilot billing generates sticker shock for engineering teams. On the security front, three actively exploited zero-days — including a Windows Netlogon flaw enabling unauthenticated domain controller takeover — and a doubling of third-party SaaS breach involvement demand immediate attention from IAM and platform teams. Allegion's ISC West showcase and the formal Aliro 1.0 launch mark a pivotal inflection point for mobile credential interoperability, with Apple Wallet and Google Wallet now carrying Schlage NFC credentials across commercial, campus, and residential deployments.
item.itunes:summary: This week's tech landscape is shaped by three converging forces: massive AI capital consolidation, a landmark shift in enterprise AI leadership, and an accelerating identity-centric threat environment. Anthropic's confidential IPO filing arrives alongside its historic milestone of overtaking OpenAI in business adoption (34.4% vs. 32.3%), even as 79% of organizations report AI adoption challenges and GitHub's new usage-based Copilot billing generates sticker shock for engineering teams. On the security front, three actively exploited zero-days — including a Windows Netlogon flaw enabling unauthenticated domain controller takeover — and a doubling of third-party SaaS breach involvement demand immediate attention from IAM and platform teams. Allegion's ISC West showcase and the formal Aliro 1.0 launch mark a pivotal inflection point for mobile credential interoperability, with Apple Wallet and Google Wallet now carrying Schlage NFC credentials across commercial, campus, and residential deployments.
